title: Greedy Algorithms and Exchange Arguments · Complexity Checkpoint
summary: Allocate rooms, bandwidth, and deadlines under competing local choices. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a greedy-design decision and proof notebook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define a locally optimal choice and the remaining subproblem precisely.
Distinguish optimal substructure from the stronger greedy-choice property.
Transform an optimal solution to include the greedy choice without worsening it.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
